# GitHub Copilot Instructions

## About This Project

This project is my personal website. I use this website mostly to blog about programming, software development, and computer science and technology in general. I like to write about topics that interest me and that I am working on both to share my knowledge and to help me remember and process what I have learned better. This website also serves as a portfolio of my work, a way for people to learn about me, and a way for potential future employers to find and learn about me.

## About This Website

- This website is hosted at https://michaelfcollins3.dev.
- This website is hosted using GitHub Pages.
- A GitHub Actions workflow is used to publish the website to GitHub Pages.
    - The workflow is triggered on pushes to the `main` branch.
    - The workflow is also triggered on a nightly schedule to publish content that is scheduled to be published at a later date.
- The website is built using [Astro](https://astro.build/).

## Astro Rules

The following rules should be followed when writing code for this website:

- Use TypeScript for all JavaScript code.
- Use standard HTML and CSS for all HTML and CSS code.
- Use Astro components when possible to encourage reusability and improve maintainability.
- Use Markdown for all blog posts and other content.
- Use frontmatter in Markdown files to specify metadata such as title, date, tags, and draft status.
